meta.purpose: Encode expert judgment for using GSAP as a production animation engine, including timelines, ScrollTrigger, React integration, plugin choice, and performance tradeoffs.
meta.load_condition: Load when the task involves GSAP implementation, timeline architecture, ScrollTrigger, or when the user is working within a React/Vue/Svelte context using GSAP.

axioms.one_sentence: Use timelines to express choreography, not scattered one-off tweens.
axioms.full_statement: Use timelines to express choreography, not scattered one-off tweens.
axioms.why: Timelines preserve sequence, reversibility, maintainability, and reviewability.

axioms.one_sentence: Framework integrations must clean up animations according to component lifecycle.
axioms.full_statement: Framework integrations must clean up animations according to component lifecycle.
axioms.why: Leaked animations cause duplicated effects and broken state after navigation.

axioms.one_sentence: ScrollTrigger should guide reading, not hijack it.
axioms.full_statement: ScrollTrigger should guide reading, not hijack it.
axioms.why: Scroll-driven motion can easily make content feel trapped or gimmicky.

axioms.one_sentence: A dropped frame destroys premium perception.
axioms.full_statement: A dropped frame destroys premium perception.
axioms.why: Smoothness is part of visual quality, not an implementation detail.

ontology.one_sentence: A structured animation sequence with coordinated tweens and labels.
ontology.essence: A structured animation sequence with coordinated tweens and labels.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'timeline' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: A scroll-linked trigger system for controlling animation progress or timing.
ontology.essence: A scroll-linked trigger system for controlling animation progress or timing.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'ScrollTrigger' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: A controlled offset across similar elements to create rhythm and hierarchy.
ontology.essence: A controlled offset across similar elements to create rhythm and hierarchy.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'stagger' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: Removing or reverting animation side effects when a component unmounts or re-renders.
ontology.essence: Removing or reverting animation side effects when a component unmounts or re-renders.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'cleanup' or describes this scenario.

Do not use scroll animation when it reduces reading comfort.
